name: S2: restore add-subject on populated path + manage/archive entry on hub
description: Two distinct UI gaps: (1) SubjectsBrowse.tsx renders the onCreateSubject button only in the empty-state branch (subjects.length===0, lines 47-65); once one or more subjects exist there is no add-subject affordance on the screen. (2) SubjectHub (apps/mobile/src/app/(app)/subject-hub/[subjectId]/index.tsx:220) renders no manage/pause/archive/delete entry point; that functionality exists only in Library (library.tsx:1278-1328, archive-first pattern). Learners cannot add a second subject from the browse list, and cannot archive or pause a subject from the hub.
acceptanceCriteria:
1. SubjectsBrowse populated path (subjects.length>0) shows an 'Add subject' button — e.g. a row at the bottom of the list or a header action — that calls onCreateSubject; testID subjects-browse-create is present in both empty and populated states.
2. SubjectHub screen exposes a manage entry — at minimum an overflow/kebab or pressable 'Manage' — that allows the owner/learner scope to pause or archive the subject following the archive-first pattern from Library.
3. The manage entry is not shown for supporter-proxy scope (canStudy-derived gating, matching SubjectHubNotesSection pattern).
4. Regression tests: SubjectsBrowse.test.tsx asserts subjects-browse-create is present when subjects.length>0; SubjectHubRoute.test.tsx asserts manage/archive entry is visible for learner scope and absent for proxy scope.
riskImpact: Learners with existing subjects cannot add a second one without knowing to navigate back through onboarding. Users wanting to pause or archive a subject have no in-context path — they must discover the Library screen, a dead-end UX gap.
